summary: Verified 24-hour grace period feature for phone number release on auto-renew insufficient funds. All 13 Jest tests pass. Code review confirms: (1) insufficient_funds branch sets _graceUntil instead of immediate release, (2) grace period is exactly 24h (24*60*60*1000 ms), (3) number stays 'active' during grace, (4) grace expiry triggers release with _releasedAfterGrace=true, (5) successful renewal clears _graceUntil in both atomic DB update and local copy, (6) buildGracePeriodMsg has 4 language variants (en/fr/zh/hi) with deposit CTA and 24h deadline, (7) admin notifications on grace start and expiry, (8) no immediate releaseFromProvider without _graceUntil check. Node.js service running, API health check passes.

tests: insufficient_funds branch sets _graceUntil instead of immediately releasing
successful renewal clears _graceUntil in atomic DB update
successful renewal clears _graceUntil in local copy
buildGracePeriodMsg function exists with 4 language variants
grace message includes deposit CTA and 24h deadline
admin notification sent on grace start
admin notification sent on grace expiry + release
no immediate release without grace period check
number stays in grace period on subsequent hourly checks
_releasedAfterGrace flag set on grace expiry release
no auto-renew without grace when autoRenew is OFF

critical_code_review_comments: Grace period logic correctly implemented: first insufficient_funds hit sets _graceUntil 24h in future, keeps status='active', sends user DM with deposit CTA
Subsequent hourly checks during grace period correctly skip release (line 191: 'still in grace period')
Grace expiry correctly triggers release with _releasedAfterGrace=true audit flag
Successful renewal (attemptAutoRenew) correctly clears _graceUntil in both atomic DB update ($set 'val.numbers.$._graceUntil': null) and local array copy
No immediate releaseFromProvider call in insufficient_funds branch without first checking _graceUntil
Non-autoRenew numbers still release immediately (no grace period for opt-out users) - this is correct behavior
buildGracePeriodMsg includes all required elements: shortfall amount, 24h deadline, deposit CTA (/start -> Deposit), reassurance that number stays active during grace

context_for_next_testing_agent: 24-hour grace period feature for phone number release is fully implemented and tested. The feature addresses production incident where user 7946200829 lost phone +18888499956 due to immediate release on insufficient funds ($80.15 balance vs $120 renewal cost). Now: (1) First insufficient_funds sets _graceUntil 24h ahead, (2) Number stays active during grace, (3) User gets urgent DM with deposit CTA in their language, (4) Admin gets 'Grace Period Started' notification, (5) Next hourly check either auto-renews if funds deposited or releases if grace expired, (6) Successful renewal clears _graceUntil. Jest tests in /app/tests/phone-scheduler-grace-period.test.js verify all logic via source code analysis.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no defects found. Grace period feature correctly implemented per spec.
